steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank must fill the end of the phrase "a more unusual ______" and connect two independent clauses. The first clause ends with "a more unusual [shade]" and the second is "deposits of crushed coral and other organic matter lend the sand a unique pink hue." The word though must serve as a transitional connector between them.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — shade, though;: A comma after shade closes the first independent clause, though serves as the transitional connector, and the semicolon after though correctly separates it from the start of the second independent clause. This matches the pattern: IC, though; IC. ✓
  • Choice B — shade, though,: A comma after though instead of a semicolon fails to properly separate though from the second independent clause — two commas around a transitional word is not standard for this construction. ✗
  • Choice C — shade, though: No punctuation after though runs the transitional word directly into the second independent clause without the required separator. ✗
  • Choice D — shade; though: Placing the semicolon before though separates the first clause from its transitional word, leaving the two independent clauses with no punctuation between though and the second clause. ✗
That matches Choice A — shade, though;.
